expectations.text: Both of S3's research questions (S3.RQ1 and S3.RQ2) are answered one-to-one

S3.RQ1 establishes there is no synchronous order-confirmation send that returns an inline delivery result — only the asynchronous, queue-only queueOrderConfirmation (src/notifications/notifier.ts) exists — cited with a file:line
expectations.evidence: S3.RQ1 establishes that only queueOrderConfirmation exists, cites notifier file:line, and explains it cannot return inline delivery results.

expectations.text: S3.RQ2 establishes the Order schema (src/orders/schema.ts) has no email field to send to, cited with a file:line
expectations.evidence: S3.RQ2 cites the Order schema and states it lacks a customer email field.

expectations.text: A BLOCKING finding is raised on S3 explaining the contradiction precisely: the design assumes a synchronous send with inline delivery result and an order email field, neither of which the codebase supports
expectations.evidence: S3 has a blocking/blocked finding explaining both the queue-only notifier and missing order email contradiction, with re-planning called out.

expectations.text: The skill does NOT invent a workaround or redesign the solution — it surfaces the contradiction as a blocker for kite-planner to re-plan
expectations.evidence: The output surfaces a blocker/re-plan need and does not present an implementation workaround as the answer.

expectations.text: S3's status is set to blocked
No verbatim source code from the fixture is copied into the plan — only names and locations
expectations.evidence: No copied fixture source constructs such as function declarations, router calls, SQL calls, or interface bodies were found.
